title: Design and implement FiniteField trait for generic field arithmetic
description:
Define a two-tier trait system for generic finite field operations. ## Tier 1: `FiniteField` (universal) Core trait for all field elements (const-generic and runtime-parameterized): - Supertraits: `Clone + PartialEq + Eq + Hash + Debug` + all arithmetic ops (`Add`, `Sub`, `Mul`, `Div`, `Neg`) with both owned and borrowed receivers - Identity: `is_zero()`, `is_one()`, `zero_like(&self)`, `one_like(&self)` â€” no freestanding constructors (runtime types cannot construct without field context) - Field info: `characteristic()` via associated type `Characteristic` (defaults to `u64`, extensible to `BigUint`), `extension_degree()` - Inversion: `inv() -> Self` ### `Wide` associated type for delayed reduction Inspired by fflas-ffpack's `MMHelper` bounds-tracking pattern. Enables generic matrix/vector kernels to accumulate multiply-add operations in a wider type and reduce only when overflow is imminent: ```rust type Wide: Clone + Add + AddAssign; fn to_wide(&self) -> Self::Wide; fn mul_to_wide(&self, rhs: &Self) -> Self::Wide; fn reduce_wide(w: Self::Wide) -> Self; fn max_unreduced_additions() -> usize; ``` For `Fp

` with `Wide = u128`: `max_unreduced_additions = (2^128 - 1) / (P - 1)^2`. For `Gf2_8` with `Wide = u16`: accumulate XORs without reduction (XOR never overflows). This is the critical enabler for performant `FieldVec::dot_product` and downstream `FieldMatrix` multiplication. ## Tier 2: `ConstField: FiniteField + Copy` Subtrait for compile-time-known fields only: - Adds freestanding `zero() -> Self`, `one() -> Self`, `order() -> u64` - Only for const-generic types (`Fp

`, `Gf2_8`, etc.), not runtime-parameterized (`Gf2mElement`) ## Tier 3: `FiniteFieldExt` (blanket extension) Default-implemented convenience methods: `square()`, `pow()`, `frobenius()`. See `dev/plans/gfpn_groundwork_analysis.md` section 4.1 for full specification.
